key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network can simultaneously predict laser welding penetration state, penetration depth, and weld seam morphology from weld-pool images and process parameters.
engineering_relevance: This enables in-situ quality control for laser penetration welding by turning real-time camera data into actionable weld-quality predictions.
method: Uses CMOS camera top-view weld pool images as the primary sensing input.
Combines convolutional neural networks with state space models to extract spatial-temporal features.
Trains a multi-task framework that jointly predicts penetration state, depth, and cross-section morphology.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm depth prediction error, and 95.65% cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on the quality and representativeness of the constructed dataset, so robustness under different materials, optics, and welding conditions still needs careful validation.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
